one_time_description: Treaty Land Entitlement (TLE) settlement of $92,526,063 and Site C settlement of $17,333,708 — both large non-recurring claim settlements received in fiscal 2023
economic_notes: Nation holds equity interests in multiple LPs and corporations including Dunne-za Ventures LP, Black Diamond Cygnus LP, West Moberly First Nations Investments Inc., WM Indigiinet Internet Service Inc., Papawewin Developments LP, Six Nations Ventures LP, Twin Sisters Native Plants Nursery LP. Operates Mountain Rain Adventures (guiding territory land tenure expiring 2039), 0944996 BC Ltd (Golf Course), West Moberly First Nation Holdings Inc. Agriculture Benefits Trust and Treaty Land Entitlement Trust funds held at Scotia Wealth Management.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Two massive one-time settlements received: TLE settlement $92.5M and Site C settlement $17.3M, causing accumulated surplus to jump from $29.6M to $118.2M. TLE trust funds of $64.8M held at Scotia Wealth Management. Treaty Loan Payable of $3.28M repaid from TLE settlement proceeds. $24.75M in TLE Settlement Distribution Payments made to members. Three entities consolidated (Holdings Inc., 0944996 BC Ltd, Mountain Rain Adventures); eight entities accounted for by modified equity method. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements provided. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $4,535,896 + FNHA $546,297 + CMHC $88,965 + Province of BC $24,839,111 = $30,010,269; osr is total revenue minus government transfers including settlements, trust, industrial, interest and other. expenses_enterprises covers West Moberly First Nation Holdings Inc. $2,622,417 + 0944996 BC Ltd $324,410 + Mountain Rain Adventures $240,392 = $3,186,829.
